content:
🚀 claude-blog | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To automate and enhance the production of high-quality, SEO-optimized blog content using AI, ensuring it meets stringent quality and platform-specific requirements.
✨ Key Features: • AI-powered blog content creation, optimization, and auditing • Dual optimization for Google rankings (E-E-A-T) and AI citations
📖 Summary: The claude-blog repository provides a comprehensive suite of AI-powered tools for writing, optimizing, and auditing blog content. It aims to produce high-quality articles that are dual-optimized for search engine rankings and AI citation platforms, ensuring content meets specific quality standards through a multi-stage delivery contract.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 storyboarder | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Rapid visualization and iteration of story ideas through digital storyboarding.
✨ Key Features: • Simple, Smooth Drawing • Super Simple Interface • Printed Worksheets • Works with Wacom • Easy Organization
📖 Summary: Storyboarder is a JavaScript application designed to help creators visualize stories quickly through simple drawing tools. It allows users to create storyboards from scratch or by importing Fountain screenplays, facilitating rapid iteration and sharing of visual ideas without the complexity of professional animation software.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 PhantomBot | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide streamers with an interactive and customizable Twitch chat bot for channel moderation, viewer engagement, and entertainment.
✨ Key Features: • Built-in webpanel • Enhanced moderation • Games • Point system • Raffles
📖 Summary: PhantomBot is an open-source, Java-powered Twitch bot designed to enhance live streams with interactive features and robust moderation. It offers a built-in web panel for easy management, a customizable point system, games, raffles, and integration with popular streaming services.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 GLM-OCR | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Accurate and fast optical character recognition (OCR) for complex documents, including tables, formulas, and information extraction.
✨ Key Features: • State-of-the-Art Performance on document understanding benchmarks • Optimized for Real-World Scenarios (complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals) • Efficient Inference with 0.9B parameters, supporting vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama
📖 Summary: GLM-OCR is a powerful multimodal OCR model designed for complex document understanding. It leverages a GLM-V architecture with advanced training techniques like Multi-Token Prediction and reinforcement learning to achieve state-of-the-art accuracy and efficiency. The model is optimized for real-world scenarios and offers easy integration through an open-source SDK.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 zot | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a compliant and robust registry for storing and serving OCI container images and artifacts.
✨ Key Features: • OCI-native container image registry • Production-ready • Vendor-neutral • Purely based on OCI Distribution Specification • Stores images in OCI image format
📖 Summary: zot is a production-ready, vendor-neutral OCI-native container image registry. It strictly adheres to the OCI Image Format and Distribution Specification, ensuring compatibility and interoperability without proprietary extensions.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
